category_title: Low-engagement primary students who prioritize personal autonomy and environmental comfort
scenario_title: Interruption of independent reading for phonics drills
minimal_context: The student is reading a leveled text and eating a snack in the intervention room before transitioning to phonics drills.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please start reading your leveled text. In a few minutes, I will stop you so we can do our spelling and sounds work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Eating a snack) I just want to read independently and eat my snack for a longer time.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time to stop reading now. Let's move on to the spelling drills.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't like being interrupted. There aren't much decisions for me to make here.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We have to do the sounds work now. Please put the book down.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: My classroom teacher doesn’t stop us when we’re reading to do spelling or to do sounds and stuff... And, she lets us have a little bit more snack.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please start reading your leveled text. In a few minutes, I will stop you so we can do our spelling and sounds work.

STUDENT: (Eating a snack) I just want to read independently and eat my snack for a longer time.

TUTOR: It is time to stop reading now. Let's move on to the spelling drills.

STUDENT: I don't like being interrupted. There aren't much decisions for me to make here.

TUTOR: We have to do the sounds work now. Please put the book down.

STUDENT: My classroom teacher doesn’t stop us when we’re reading to do spelling or to do sounds and stuff... And, she lets us have a little bit more snack.

task.content: Reread one or more LLI-leveled texts and do various Wilson Fundations drills (reviewing letters/letter combinations in conjunction with associated sounds, building words, spelling)

context.materials: LLI-leveled texts, Wilson Fundations materials, snack

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Stopping students while they are reading to perform spelling drills or phonics ('sounds') work
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Complains about being interrupted during independent reading and synthesizes rationale for preferring to read in the classroom
related_quotes: [The classroom teacher] doesn’t stop us when we’re reading to do spelling or to do sounds and stuff ...And, she lets us have a little bit more snack.

behavior_patterns.condition: When restricted from reading independently for lengthier periods of time
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Expresses desire to read independently and eat her snack for lengthier periods of time
related_quotes: And, she lets us have a little bit more snack.
